blockers.title: Reports view violates the aggregate-only requirement by fetching individual rows
blockers.evidence: Spec H18 requires reports to be sanctioned aggregates only, with no individuals. `src/admin/views/ReportsView.tsx:29-36` calls `adminListEvents`, `adminListOpportunities`, and `listMembers` and then computes report counts in the browser. Those backend queries return row-level objects: event IDs/titles/audience at `convex/admin/events.ts:82-107`, opportunity IDs/titles/audience at `convex/admin/opportunities.ts:104-138`, and member IDs/names/lane/standing/country at `convex/admin/members.ts:116-198`. The UI may render aggregates, but the Reports page still sends individual records to the client.
blockers.required_fix: Move every report metric behind backend aggregate-only queries and make `ReportsView` consume only aggregate responses. Do not call row-listing admin endpoints from the reports route.

blockers.title: Event audience lane can be changed after bookings, breaking under-18 isolation
blockers.evidence: Spec A2 says youth lane sees only `audience_lane=youth`, and the vault under-18 decision requires minors to be switched off from adult events. RSVP enforces the lane only at booking time in `convex/events.ts:269-277`. Admin edit accepts `audience_lane` for existing published/postponed events and patches it without checking registrations at `convex/admin/events.ts:189-205` and `convex/admin/events.ts:321-328`. Admin registration and check-in then operate on all registrations for the event without revalidating member lane at `convex/admin/events.ts:583-603` and `convex/admin/events.ts:638-667`. A youth event with minor registrations can be edited into an adult event, and those minor registrations can still be listed and marked attended/no-show.
blockers.required_fix: Freeze `audience_lane` once an event has live registrations or is published, or atomically revalidate/cancel incompatible registrations with audit and notification. Add tests for youth-to-adult and adult-to-youth edits with existing registrations and check-in attempts.

blockers.title: Opportunity audience can be narrowed after applications, bypassing women_only and safeguarding gates
blockers.evidence: Spec B5-B8 says `women_only` excludes allies and minors/restricted_unknown never see adult opportunities server-side. The member apply path enforces lane/audience at `convex/opportunities.ts:88-103` and `convex/opportunities.ts:245-253`, but admin edit can replace an existing open opportunity's `audience` without checking existing applications at `convex/admin/opportunities.ts:206-221` and `convex/admin/opportunities.ts:261-296`. `listApplications` returns every application for the listing at `convex/admin/opportunities.ts:165-191`, and `recordResult` can mark any received/shortlisted application won/lost without rechecking the member against the current audience at `convex/admin/opportunities.ts:475-525`. `myApplications` also returns opportunity title/state/notes for all of a member's historical applications without any lane or audience re-check at `convex/opportunities.ts:375-407`. An ally can apply while a listing is `open`, then remain in a `women_only` pool after an edit and still receive results.
blockers.required_fix: Freeze `audience` once an opportunity is open or has applications, or block narrowing when incompatible applications exist. Re-check lane/audience in member application history and admin application/result mutations. Add regression tests for open-to-women_only with ally applications and for a member later corrected to minor/restricted.

non_blocking_notes: `npm run typecheck` passed. `npm run check` and targeted Vitest could not run in this read-only Gate 4 sandbox because Astro/Vitest attempted to write generated temp files.

spec_compliance.criterion: A2-A3 events lane gating, registrations, and check-in
spec_compliance.evidence: Initial RSVP is lane-gated, but existing registrations survive an admin `audience_lane` edit and can still be processed by admin check-in.

spec_compliance.criterion: B5-B8 opportunity eligibility, women_only exclusion, and server-side visibility
spec_compliance.evidence: Initial apply is gated, but live `audience` edits and application/result/history paths do not revalidate eligibility.
ReportsView fetches row-level members, events, and opportunities to compute report numbers client-side.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand locks for added member-facing strings
spec_compliance.evidence: No member-facing em dash blocker found in the reviewed diff; the blocking issues are behavioral/security compliance gaps.

summary: The slice cannot ship. The implementation has real server-side/spec gaps around aggregate-only reporting, under-18 event isolation after admin edits, and opportunity audience eligibility after live edits.
